title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new smart home thermostat they’ve installed.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’m finally getting the hang of the 'Smart-Temp' system. It’s great being able to turn the heating on from my phone. || Woman: It’s convenient. But [1]I’ve found the 'auto-learn' feature a bit unpredictable[/1]. It kept turning the heating off when I was still in the living room just because I wasn't moving much. || Man: I had the same issue! I ended up just setting a manual schedule. || Woman: [2]I was pleasantly surprised by the detailed energy reports it generates, though[/2]. It’s helped me identify which rooms are losing the most heat. || Man: I agree, the data is very eye-opening. Though the unit itself is a bit of an eyesore on the wall. || Woman: It is a bit clunky, but I suppose functionality comes first in this case.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’m finally getting the hang of the 'Smart-Temp' system. It’s great being able to turn the heating on from my phone.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s convenient. But I’ve found the 'auto-learn' feature a bit unpredictable. It kept turning the heating off when I was still in the living room just because I wasn't moving much.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I had the same issue! I ended up just setting a manual schedule.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I was pleasantly surprised by the detailed energy reports it generates, though. It’s helped me identify which rooms are losing the most heat.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree, the data is very eye-opening. Though the unit itself is a bit of an eyesore on the wall.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is a bit clunky, but I suppose functionality comes first in this case.

questions.question: The woman complains that the thermostat's automated feature

options.B: fails to detect her presence accurately.
options.C: uses more energy than the manual setting.

questions.explanation: She notes it turned off while she was in the room because she 'wasn't moving much' [1].

questions.question: What did the woman find most beneficial about the device?

options.B: the information it provides on heat loss
options.C: the ease of the mobile interface

questions.explanation: She was surprised by the 'energy reports' that help identify heat loss in rooms [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new city-wide car-pooling initiative.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, are you going to sign up for the 'Commute-Share' scheme? It’s supposed to cut traffic in half. || Woman: [3]I suspect it’ll be a struggle to find people whose schedules actually align with mine[/3]. I often have to stay late at short notice. || Man: That is the tricky part. But the app is meant to handle flexible timings. || Woman: [4]I’ve heard that the designated car-pool lanes are already becoming as congested as the regular ones[/4], which seems to defeat the purpose. || Man: I agree. If everyone switches, the advantage disappears unless they increase the number of lanes. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a good idea in theory, but the infrastructure just isn't there to support it properly yet.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, are you going to sign up for the 'Commute-Share' scheme? It’s supposed to cut traffic in half.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect it’ll be a struggle to find people whose schedules actually align with mine. I often have to stay late at short notice.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That is the tricky part. But the app is meant to handle flexible timings.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve heard that the designated car-pool lanes are already becoming as congested as the regular ones, which seems to defeat the purpose.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. If everyone switches, the advantage disappears unless they increase the number of lanes.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a good idea in theory, but the infrastructure just isn't there to support it properly yet.

questions.question: The woman is hesitant to join the scheme because of

options.A: safety concerns regarding the other drivers.
options.B: the unpredictability of her working hours.
options.C: the high cost of the monthly membership.

questions.explanation: She fears it's hard to find people when she has to 'stay late at short notice' [3].

questions.question: Both speakers agree that the initiative's effectiveness is limited by

options.A: the lack of people willing to participate.
options.B: the poor quality of the mobile application.
options.C: the current state of the road system.

questions.explanation: They agree that the 'lanes are already becoming congested' and 'infrastructure isn't there' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a recently opened boutique hotel.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I stayed at the 'Lighthouse Inn' last weekend. The views of the coast from the bedroom were incredible. || Woman: [5]I found the service in the restaurant to be a bit overly formal and stiff[/5], which didn't really fit the seaside vibe. || Man: I see what you mean. It felt a bit like they were trying too hard to be 'high-end'. || Woman: [6]I did think the soundproofing in the rooms was exceptionally good, though[/6]. I couldn't hear the storm outside at all. || Man: I agree, it was very peaceful. Though I was a bit surprised they didn't have any tea-making facilities in the room. || Woman: That is a basic oversight for a hotel at that price point. You’d think a kettle wouldn't be too much to ask for.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I stayed at the 'Lighthouse Inn' last weekend. The views of the coast from the bedroom were incredible.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the service in the restaurant to be a bit overly formal and stiff, which didn't really fit the seaside vibe.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It felt a bit like they were trying too hard to be 'high-end'.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did think the soundproofing in the rooms was exceptionally good, though. I couldn't hear the storm outside at all.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree, it was very peaceful. Though I was a bit surprised they didn't have any tea-making facilities in the room.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That is a basic oversight for a hotel at that price point. You’d think a kettle wouldn't be too much to ask for.

questions.question: What did the woman find disappointing about her experience?

options.A: the quality of the food provided
options.B: the attitude of the waiting staff
options.C: the location of the hotel restaurant

questions.explanation: She found the service 'overly formal and stiff', which didn't fit the vibe [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the hotel's
questions.explanation: They both agree that the 'soundproofing' (insulation) was exceptionally good and peaceful [6].
